title: prunella vulgaris subsp. parviflora
intro: Prunella vulgaris subsp. parviflora, also known as small-flowered self-heal, is a subspecies of the self-heal plant, a member of the mint family native to Europe, Asia, and North Africa. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its antiviral, antibacterial, and anti-inflammatory properties, making it effective in treating respiratory infections, wounds, and skin issues. The health benefits of Prunella vulgaris subsp. parviflora include its ability to boost the immune system, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antioxidant properties. Herbal preparations of this plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to support respiratory health, soothe sore throats, and promote wound healing.
